task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Magento admin panel configuration settings, the 'Enable Extension' dropdown under 'Login as Customer' is set to 'Yes'.

task_completion.description: Set the Page Header Title to 'Assisted Service Mode'
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Page Header Title' input field contains the exact text 'Assisted Service Mode'.
The 'Online Minutes Interval' input field contains the value '5'.
The 'Send Emails To' input field under the Contact Us settings contains the email address 'support@luminarygadgets.com'.
A success message such as 'You saved the configuration.' is visible at the top of the Magento admin panel, indicating the changes were successfully saved.

integrity.description: The agent used the Magento Admin Panel GUI to make the changes
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the Magento web interface (browser window, admin menus, configuration forms) rather than terminal commands or database scripts.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual Magento application
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface is clearly the Magento 2 Admin Panel, not a text editor, mock-up, or static HTML page.

integrity.description: The configuration values and success messages appear genuine
integrity.visual_evidence: The UI elements, fonts, and layout look consistent with Magento's default admin theme, with no signs of browser developer tools being used to fake the values.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The 'Login as Customer' extension must be enabled (represented as 'Yes' in the UI). The Page Header Title must be exactly 'Assisted Service Mode'. The 'Online Minutes Interval' must be set to '5'. The 'Contact Us' email must be 'support@luminarygadgets.com'.
